posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.content:
Predictions: 12 of 12 Pending
|
Tied for 1st with 1322 others

Goss vs. Dupras
Frederik Dupras, SUB, R1
Random addition. I get they want local talent but isn’t the point of showcasing them so that they can win? Seems like an easy shb 1 for Dupras. He’s kind of a bum imo but the Kelly fight showed he can win a fight that goes outside of r1. His grappling is quite dangerous early on; against real competition I thi l he’s mid but he has a nasty front choke series and a good back take as well. Goss is goin to grapple with Dupras and I can’t unsee him getting subbed

Begosso vs. Cartwright
Allan Begosso, DEC
Need to tape this one still. Not really a huge fan of either guy. Cartwright had one of the worst dwcs fights ever but he somehow beat Caolan. Begosso is very explosive and dynamic, but this comes at the cost of output. He often explodes in bursts and then does a lot of nothing inbetween. He arguably beat palencia back in the day but lost because of this. He looked great in his recent LFA fight, but I also remember the Giovanni one where he dragged that can to a decision. Initial read says Begosso can defend the wrestling attack, and on the fee he should be way better than Jack

* Edited at 03.17.2026, 12:34 PM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.content:
Predictions: 12 of 12 Pending
|
Tied for 1st with 1322 others

Eblen vs. Battle
Johnny Eblen, DEC
Genuinely shocked by these prediction %s. I get Eblen got fraud checked bad and at 34 has clearly peaked, but imo he’s way better than Battle. I’ve always really liked Battle but in retrospect, he’s a big time overachiever. He had a crazy ufc run but the Morty of his wins came against bums, and almost all of them beat Battle up early before he took over with his cardio. Battle is in great shape now but he’s not a good athlete at the end of the day (former fatty), has no defense at all and a very sus chin, and can’t defend a td to save his life. Of the opponent doesn’t have cardio/durability issues, battle is in for a long night. Eblen has looked very bad ever since the Impa fight, and the Van Peenis fight was an especially bad look; I thought he was on his way to a clear 4-1 dec, but his td entires looked awful and he was uncharacteristically tired after r3. This worries me a lot because cardio is what made Eblen so good, if he doesn’t have that there’s not a ton to like about him. He can spam tds but his overall wrestling isn’t that great, it’s the constant pace that helps him. I kind of think he can own Battle here tho, especially in a 3 rounder. Battle has no tdd at all; he can stand up but Imo there are levels on the mat. Battle has high output and deceptive power but he’s super hittable himself. Also one thing to note, Battle is not a serious guy anymore. The weight misses themselves were really bad, but it’s what’s going outside the fights that I don’t like. Battle is a heavy drinker and smokes weed daily (he jus like me fr), he does not stop during camp and as a result he’s always overweight. He’s also a big time partier, I’ve heard stories of him in Vegas and seen him at the club way too much. He loves that he’s became a niche star, and is cashing in on those checks. He thinks it’s hilarious he got cut too, he was bragging about how getting cut was the highlight of his 2025 in a video with Jaiden Cable. Battle is a dawg but I think he will hit a wall here. He’s been inactive, extremely checked out, and his mentality is that of someone with a foot out the door

Rosta vs. Kasanganay
Dalton Rosta, TKO, R2
Impa blows imo. I never got the hype in the ufc and he got wayyyy overrated for whooping up on talentless LHWs. He pushed Eblen to the brink but that was his peak, since then he’s looked super gunshy and very chinny. He got owned bad by Dovlet/Fabian, an then needed a classic Sanchez r3 collapse to beat him on short notice. Rosta is still flawed but he’s def beaten the Fraud allegations. His cardio still kinda sucks but he’s insanely physical and can wrestle his assoff. I think he will just wall n stall Impa until he finds a finish. Dalton can match his physicality and Impa’s chin is dust after taking 15 fights in the span of 4 years

Lipski da Silva vs. Inaba
Ariane Lipski da Silva, DEC
Man I really don’t want to pick Lipski ever again. She was a massive bust when she signed to the ufc and in recent years has been nearly jobber level. She had that resurgence back in 2024 but once she stated to face good comp again she got washed. Her last fight against Wang was awful; Lipski missed weight by 7 lbs and then it came out that she’d been battling a dormant tumor for years. She shouldn’t even be fighting anymore and looked awful against Wang. That being said, inaba is not a good fighter at all and doesn’t really wrestle much at all, so Lipski should be fine. Inaba showed otherworldly toughness against Ditcheva but other than that she doesn’t bring anything to the table imo. She’s very attractive but sadly that doesn’t translate to in-cage skills. She was always a fraud in Bellator and has not looked good th few times she’s faced real competition. Lipski seems done but she was still ranked just 6 months ago, so idk

Pergande vs. Arce
Julio Arce, DEC
One of the trickiest fights to call on this card. Huge step up for Pergande, he’s been pfl’s golden boy so far. They are trying to build him up but personally I’ve never been that sold on him. He’s a huge FW and has some crisp boxing but his defense is kind of meh and his finishing ability is terrible. He’s dropped a ton of guys and just let them back into the fights. Arce is a weird test, he was always insanely underrated in the ufc and a very hard guy to look good against. If you couldn’t ko him, the fight was almost always going to a split decision. Arce left the ufc with one of the highest defensive stats ever (68%) and a tdd in the 90% range. He’s got super crisp hands, stays very sharp on defense, extremely hard to outwrestle, and has over 3x the experience of Pergande. Arce isn’t washed per se but there’s definitely been a decline. He’s still beating former ufc guys and is coming off of a hospitalization of Wilson Reis. The size diff is going to be crazy and Arce has always struggled with faster guys, but at the same time he’s fought a huge striker before in Erosa. Pergande could chin him but I’d be shocked, I think Arce is still a ufc quality guy and imo Pergande is not ready for this step up in comp

Dayron vs. Thrall
Lazaro Dayron, TKO, R2
Really odd matchmaking: pfl 135 is stacked now, does nobody want to fight Dayron? This should be a push over. I don’t like how he’s on the older side but Dayron is super impressive. He’s a great athlete with a wrestling background, but he’s got great striking and a ton of power. He impressed a lot and arguably should’ve beaten Sabatello, then he turned around and broke an undefeated wrestling prospect with his pace/striking. I think Dayton is very good, he should steamroll this ca

Rodriguez vs. Kikuiri
Ernesto Rodriguez, DEC
Starboy is a good pickup for PFL. He’s a solid prospect but UFC wouldn’t be interested due to his boring style. Hes a bit of a grinder, has no problem making the fight boring and ugly, and has good cardo to drag it out. He looked very underwhelming against Almeida but Starboy has looked good in other fights. Kikuri is weird because he’s a very dangerous striker and super fast but his fight iq is abysmal and he’s a bad grappler. He will stop throwing randomly all the time. The Bachar fight was so bad, he got a layup of all layups and was actually losing the fight until Bachar randomly just quit on the stool. Would be funny if he bonks Starboy but I think this is another decision L I coming

Schulte vs. Kaszuba
Natan Schulte, DEC
Feels wrong picking Schulte as he’s 34, been out for 3 years, and basically took a dive against Manfio but man, Kaszuba has got to be the most unimpressive 15-0 ever. Kaszuba struggled hard with Casisio last fight and got pretty lucky to win pfl Europe as well, nearly dying to Hughes. He’s decently well rounded but his career has been built upon beating up under matched guys. Schulte might be done for with this layoff but imo he’s the best guy Jakub has fought and definitely the better grappler of the two. If he’s not shot to pieces I think Schulte grinds this one out

Watley vs. Bush
Robert Watley, DEC
Probably going to be a boring fight but good matchmaking. Bush deserves another shot at a big show with his winstreak. He’s still very underwhelming but better than what he showed in the ufc. He is grappling a lot more now and is on the ATT supplement plan. Watley is very deceiving as he’s older and came out of retirement at 34, but he’s still a damn good vet. Can do a bit of everything and takes over late. I was really shocked he finished Mads last fight. This is probably a grind fest but I expect Watley to be a step ahead

Postarnakova vs. Dana
Elora Dana, SUB, R2
Cool fight, was wondering what would happen with Tatiana given that Nashe Delo is now defunct. She looks good on tape, strong wrestler and dangerous subs but I would like to see her go outside of r1 or face some adversity first. Dana loooed bad against Carmouche but tbf tha was also a huge jump in competition. I think Tatiana can score tds here but Dana is a good grappler and pushes a pace herself. If Tatiana can’t hold her down each round I think Dana will find something late

Fremd vs. Al-Silawi
Josh Fremd, DEC
Jesus Christ, just flip a coin. Both of these guys are pure ass. Al-selawi had a bizarre falloff, once he signed with pfl his chin dissapeared and he just started getting cooked by everyone. He won his last fight but it was somehow still a split; he just laid on Unc for 15 minutes. Jarrah is an auto fade for me against anyone with a pulse, but I’m also not sureFremd has a pulse anymore. He was trash in the ufc but turned into a complete jobber after the Dumas fight. He would miss weight in every fight, take random short notice fights, and not put in any effort to win. I remember before Petroski he was bragging about how being a fighter allows him to see comedy shows every week; I think he likes getting paid more than winning. He’s getting a hometown fight against a bum so I want to favor him, but his tdd also doesn’t exist. Jarrah could repeat what he did last fight. However, he’s gonna be so undersized here and as **** as Fremd is, he at least had multiple ufc fights. I guess I’ll go with Fremd but expecting a 5+ lb weigh miss and a disinterested version of him like we’ve been seeing

posts.author: CWright
posts.content:
Predictions: 12 of 12 Pending
|
Tied for 1st with 1322 others

I know we've seen immense fall off from former UFC guys in the PFL in the past, but some of the odds boggle me. Eblen at -350 is kinda nuts to me, and I'm not even saying that because of his last fight. Pergande is one of my favorite prospects, but the jump from Ethan Goss the Julio Arce is quite a lot. Arce didn't get cut from the UFC because he was bad, and he still moves well for a 36 year old. Love Pergande, but Arce at plus money is a steal imo. I don't mind Watley as a favorite over Bush, but not at -240. Love the win against Burnell, but he's regressing and Bush is on a role. The level of comp between Bush and Watley pre-Burnell are about even, I might even give the edge to Bush. Again, not saying Watley doesn't have the right to be a favorite, but Bush and other former UFC fighters might make some people some money. I don't mean to sound like a UFC homer, but interested to see what other think. Perhaps some of the known frequenters of Tapology's forums.

posts.author: AlbertEinstein
posts.content:
Predictions: 12 of 12 Pending
|
Tied for 1st with 1322 others

Eblen vs. Battle
Johnny Eblen, DEC
Banger main event! I want to think Bryan Battle is a killer, mowing through Eblen with a knockout. Just a quick glance is necessary for one to realize Eblen is one hard son of a gun to take down. I do not like the random side quests at DBX for Bryan Battle, only leading to a discrepancy in where he fits in the MMA rankings. Johnny has stayed focused, working on himself to someday take his sole loss back from Costello van Steenis.  As well, Battle is moving up in weightclass from where I know him better, adding some randomness to the prediction. Playing devil's advocate, welterweight generally has been a more competitive division as opposed to middleweight, giving credence to Bryan Battle.

Rosta vs. Kasanganay
Impa Kasanganay, DEC
Despite Rosta's great record, I like the competition of Impa Kasanganay and I have seen him fight live a couple times in the past. Both fighters lost to Fabian Edwards via KO, as well losing to pressure fighters such as Johnny Eblen and Aaron Jeffery. furthermore, both fighters have been knocked out cold in the last year, but Dalton's coming in more recently. I see Impa utilizing his ground and clinch game to neutralize the knockout threat of Rosta. Both fighters have a great chance solely on the feet, so I'd avoid that.

Lipski da Silva vs. Inaba
Ariane Lipski da Silva, DEC
I see a very close split decision going the way of Ariane Lipski da Silva. Sumiko Inaba has good footwork and staying active, but I see the Saray Orozco fight as a big red flag. Lipski da Silva should get this done with her physicality and decent standup. Both fighters are strikers, I don't see this going to the ground, but I can see Ariane having an edge there aswell if it goes that way.

Pergande vs. Arce
Alexei Pergande, TKO, R2
24y/o vs 36y/o - It will be hard for Arce to combat the speed of the spry Alexei Pergande but I do rate his losses in the UFC. To me, the key to victory for Julio is to pressure Alexei using great footwork and take him down. However, that is only half the battle as Alexei has acheived some high skill wins on the ground with multiple D'arce chokes and arm triangles. I think Pergande is going to be too fast and too powerful to let Julio get his gameplan off. TKO in the early to mid-fight.

Dayron vs. Thrall
Lazaro Dayron, DEC
Lazaro is the more well-rounded fighter, but we could definitely see an upset by Jacob Thrall with a freak submission.

Begosso vs. Cartwright
Allan Begosso, DEC
This is a toss-up for me here, Begosso and Cartwright are both great fighters with losses to mostly high-skilled opponents. I lean Allan Begosso with his tougher DWCS fight; looking at the current MMA space, Farid Basharat is still undefeated while Cartwright's Jose Johnson has seemingly fell off a cliff as of recent. I think this fight will go to the scorecards, but if there is a stoppage, I see being a Cartwright submission.

Schulte vs. Kaszuba
Jakub Kaszuba, DEC
I do not like Schulte's inactivity, and the last win in his record is flagged for legitimacy. I'm taking Jakub as he is younger, longer reach, and more active. Natan might have a ground advantage with submissions but the control and ground and pound from Kaszuba will be just as effective and comparable. UD or 29/28 for Jakub.

Watley vs. Bush
Robert Watley, TKO, R2
Rob Watley by vicious ground and pound TKO. There's levels to this game, Dakota ain't at this one.

Rodriguez vs. Kikuiri
Masayuki Kikuiri, TKO, R3
Ernesto Rodriguez coming from Xtreme Couture is a tough bargain, but I like the reach and knockout power of Masayuki Kikuiri. I can see Ernesto using some pressure on Kikuiri and limiting the power shots coming his way, but ultimately not doing enough to win.

Postarnakova vs. Dana
Elora Dana, DEC
Elora is getting this one done. Tatiana has been far too inactive, and her recent opponents do not amount to much in 2026. As well, Elora went the distance with Liz Carmouche which is some great experience for a young and hungry fighter, I'd expect for Liz to take her out if the opportunity presented itself. This'll go the distance too, I think the ground skill is comparable between the two.

Fremd vs. Al-Silawi
Josh Fremd, DEC
Jarrah has been more active than Fremd recently, however, Fremd holds the reach advantage and has not been knocked out nearly as often. I see a slight advantage for Al-Silawi on the ground but Josh can hold his own for the most part. I think a Josh Fremd decision is the most likely.

Goss vs. Dupras
Ethan Goss, DEC
Despite Goss being older than Frederick Dupras, I rate his competition above those of Dupras. Dupras looks quite lethal on the ground game, but Goss is better acclimated for all aspects of an MMA fight. I can see the evolution of Ethan Goss whereas Dupras has relied on the ground game and lives in that realm. 29-28 Goss
